mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding its 4-quarter moving average signals expanding aggregate demand, which improves corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansionary GDP periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the federal funds rate compresses equity valuations by raising the discount rate applied to future cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns when the monthly rate hike exceeds 25 basis points.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the rate hike signal, or the coefficient on FEDFUNDS changes in a linear regression remains statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous rises in the unemployment rate and declines in total nonfarm payrolls indicate labor market deterioration, which precedes earnings recessions and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of historical instances, or the interaction term in a multivariate return model yields a p-value greater than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Surges in the industrial production index reflect rebounding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which reduces input cost pressures and supports equity multiples over the following week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns do not occur in at least 55% of INDPRO surge events, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for VIX levels.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts indicate strengthening consumer confidence and durable goods demand, which translates into higher corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The Spearman rank correlation between monthly HOUST changes and 60-day forward SPX returns remains below 0.05 over a 10-year sample, or forward returns are flat during housing booms.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.0 percent, it signals a tight labor market that boosts wage-driven consumer spending, thereby lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative or flat in > 50% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 4.0%, or the signal's information ratio drops below 0.2 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand expansion, which accelerates earnings growth expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to yield positive 20-day forward returns in more than 45% of co-occurring surge events, or the interaction coefficient in a factor model is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price inflation above 3.0 percent erodes real corporate margins and forces aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day forward returns in > 55% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0% YoY, or the forward return differential between high-inflation and low-inflation regimes is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05).
